body: The bass multiplier sets how long low frequencies ring relative to the rest of the tail. Shortening the lows keeps a big reverb from turning boomy on a mix bus; lengthening them adds warmth to pads and vocals.

Mid/side processing lets the reverb sit wider on the stereo edges while keeping the centre clear, so a lead or vocal stays present. Tempo-synced LFO modulation of the reverb time adds subtle organic movement.
